complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it presents a realistic scenario requiring interpretation of a medical condition and decision-making about appropriate assessment and intervention sequencing rather than simple recall of definitions.
construct_actually_tested: Comprehensive re-entry assessment and planning procedures following traumatic brain injury in pediatric populations

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires understanding how different school professionals collaborate on medical management within an educational context, applying knowledge to a specific student scenario.
construct_actually_tested: Shared responsibility and professional coordination in diabetes management and monitoring within school settings

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it asks students to retrieve specific knowledge about the characteristic educational profile most commonly associated with FASD without requiring interpretation or decision-making.
construct_actually_tested: Executive functioning deficits and average intellectual ability in fetal alcohol spectrum disorder presentations

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires analyzing how a specific seizure type affects educational processes and distinguishing this from misconceptions about severity and behavioral effects.
construct_actually_tested: Educational impact of absence seizures on sustained attention and classroom learning accumulation processes

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires students to identify the specific term and concept for cognitive changes following chemotherapy, a factual retrieval task without scenario interpretation.
construct_actually_tested: Cancer-related cognitive impairment as distinct etiology in chemotherapy survivors following post-treatment recovery

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires analyzing which health conditions during specific developmental windows are most likely to cause intellectual disability, comparing mechanisms and developmental timing.
construct_actually_tested: Critical developmental periods and identification of neurological threats to intellectual functioning capacity

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it asks students to identify the definition and purpose of 504 plans versus IDEA special education, requiring retrieval of categorical distinctions without complex scenario analysis.
construct_actually_tested: Distinction between 504 plan eligibility criteria and IDEA special education disability category requirements

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it presents a complex presentation requiring interpretation of how psychological factors interact with medical conditions and selection of appropriate evidence-based interventions.
construct_actually_tested: Pain-related anxiety and avoidance behaviors in medical conditions addressed through cognitive-behavioral intervention strategies

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires retrieving established knowledge about how ADHD affects educational outcomes regardless of cognitive ability, without requiring scenario interpretation or complex synthesis.
construct_actually_tested: ADHD impact on academic achievement and learning outcomes independent of intellectual ability level

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires identifying the appropriate professional response to a chronic medical condition affecting school attendance and performance, distinguishing appropriate from inappropriate exclusionary practices.
construct_actually_tested: Professional coordination with medical team for chronic sickle cell disease management in school settings

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires recognizing the connection between corticosteroid medication effects and behavior changes, then applying knowledge about appropriate professional communication and coordination.
construct_actually_tested: Recognition of medication side effects as behavioral etiology and implementation of physician communication protocols

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires understanding that standard assessment procedures are inappropriate and determining which modifications (braille, large print, audio format) maintain assessment validity.
construct_actually_tested: Identification and implementation of assessment modification procedures for students with visual impairment populations

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires retrieving factual knowledge about the neurobiological nature of tics and their relationship to stress without requiring complex scenario analysis or decision-making.
construct_actually_tested: Understanding involuntary nature of tics in Tourette syndrome and stress-exacerbation relationships in educational contexts

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires identifying the correct IDEA disability category definition and matching it to a health condition, a factual retrieval task without complex interpretive judgment.
construct_actually_tested: IDEA disability category classification and matching of health conditions affecting school attendance and functioning

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires interpreting reported symptoms, recognizing them as medication side effects, and determining appropriate professional action involving communication with the prescribing physician.
construct_actually_tested: Recognition of medication side effects and implementation of appropriate physician reporting protocols for psychostimulants

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires identifying a medical emergency situation, distinguishing it from behavioral problems, and applying appropriate emergency response protocols rather than punitive approaches.
construct_actually_tested: Recognition of hypoglycemic medical emergency situations and implementation of appropriate emergency management protocols

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires analyzing how prenatal drug exposure affects multiple cognitive and behavioral domains, distinguishing comprehensive assessment approaches from single-domain assessments.
construct_actually_tested: Analysis of multiple neurodevelopmental domains affected by prenatal drug exposure in neuropsychological assessment procedures

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires retrieving factual knowledge about which medication classes have cognitive side effects, specifically naming anticonvulsants without requiring complex clinical interpretation.
construct_actually_tested: Identification of cognitive side effects of anticonvulsant medications on processing speed and learning capacity

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires understanding the neuropsychological basis for accommodation decisions and distinguishing legitimate compensatory adjustments from grade-lowering or academic-avoidance practices.
construct_actually_tested: Extended test time accommodation rationale based on processing speed and working memory constraints in ADHD

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires interpreting post-illness functioning, recognizing anxiety as a normal response to medical trauma, and determining appropriate graduated re-integration rather than immediate or indefinite homebound placement.
construct_actually_tested: Development of gradual re-entry planning and accommodation needs following severe illness and hospitalization recovery
